# QORM

> QORM (Queryable Object Rendering Model) is an agent-native, declarative-UI
> runtime. A QORM app is a small, language-neutral JSON bundle (manifest + scenes
> + actions + an optional Go middle-layer) that one pure-Go runtime renders live
> in a browser, snapshots to static HTML, compiles + ed25519-signs into a
> verifiable bundle, delivers over-the-air with rollback, packages as
> web / iOS / Android / desktop, and exposes to AI agents over MCP. No cgo — it
> cross-compiles everywhere.

Dual-consumer by design: every artifact is readable, installable, and writable by
a human developer, and discoverable, derivable, and self-verifiable by an AI
agent. Docs marked "auto-generated" are regenerated from the code and guarded by
tests — trust them over prose. Write apps in the format the runtime accepts
**today**: follow getting-started + the `examples/` + the auto-generated
references below (the runnable sources are the contract).

## Start here

- [README](README.md) — what QORM is; run / build / sign / package at a glance.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) — a short orientation for AI agents working in this repo.
- [Getting started](docs/tutorials/getting-started.md) — install, first app, the run loop.
- [First scene](docs/tutorials/first-scene.md) · [First action](docs/tutorials/first-action.md) · [First component](docs/tutorials/first-component.md)

## The format (write apps in this)

- [examples/](examples) — the canonical runnable patterns: text via `text`, bind with `{{state.x}}`, `onPress` names an action in `actions/`, components in `qorm.json` with `{{prop.x}}`.
- [Widget catalog](docs/reference/widgets.md) — every node `type` the renderer accepts (auto-generated from `internal/render`, guarded by `TestWidgetCatalogInSync`).
- [Capabilities](docs/platforms/capabilities.md) — built-in hardware/OS ops, callbacks, and platforms (auto-generated from `internal/capability`).

## Middle-layer — add your own native ops

- [User middle-layer](docs/platforms/native-middlelayer.md) — one `native/desktop.go`; register ops with `qormext.Register`, reach hardware with `qormext.Native`, push UI events with `qormext.Emit`. Compiles into the desktop binary AND the mobile/web WASM.
- [examples/middleware](examples/middleware) · [examples/native-ext](examples/native-ext)

## Platforms

- [Platform support matrix](docs/platforms/support-matrix.md) — what works where, at a glance.
- [Mobile](docs/platforms/mobile.md) · [Desktop](docs/platforms/desktop.md) · [Web](docs/platforms/web.md) · [Mini-app](docs/platforms/miniapp.md)

## Examples (runnable — the canonical patterns)

- [counter](examples/counter) · [todo](examples/todo) · [tasks](examples/tasks) — state + actions + lists
- [dashboard](examples/dashboard) — data-viz widgets · [gallery](examples/gallery) — the widget gallery
- [hardware](examples/hardware) · [desktop-hardware](examples/desktop-hardware) — the capability catalog exercised
- [uikit](examples/uikit) — JSON-defined custom components · [i18n](examples/i18n) — ICU messages, plurals, RTL
- [menus](examples/menus) — desktop system-bar / tray / right-click menus · [floating](examples/floating) — chromeless, custom-shape window

## Drive it as an AI agent

- [Human-AI collaboration](docs/collaboration.md) — a human and an agent on one live app over SSE + MCP, each seeing the other; the "AI edited" toast and `qorm_activity` close the loop.
- [integrations/](integrations) — drop-in MCP config + per-agent setup (Claude Code/Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf) + a QORM **skill**.
- [Agent skill](integrations/skill/SKILL.md) — the runnable format, the MCP tools, and how to self-verify.
- [MCP tools](docs/agent/mcp-tools.md) — the Model Context Protocol surface to read/edit a live app.
- [Verifying an app](docs/verification.md) — `qorm measure` / `qorm check` render the app and report real geometry, so you can self-verify your own edits against ground truth.
- CLI: `qorm new | run | render | build | keygen | verify | package | measure | check | mcp | docs | updates`.

## Trust & security

- [Bundle signing](docs/security/bundle-signing.md) — ed25519 verify-the-bundle delivery.
- [Permission model](docs/security/permission-model.md) · [Security model](docs/security/security-model.md)

## Commercial use

- [ops/TERMS.md](ops/TERMS.md) — the source is MIT; a good-faith Patreon membership covers commercial white-labeling (custom icon / removing the "Made with QORM" note).
